Container.Name: OpenScrub
Container.Overview: Local video redaction with human review. OpenScrub detects and blurs PII in videos and screen recordings — faces, license plates, names, dates of birth, phone numbers, SSNs, medical record numbers, emails, addresses, card numbers, API keys, IP addresses, plus your own custom regex categories. Scan, review every detection (one card per person), then render with blur, black-box, or mosaic. Everything runs on this server; no footage leaves your network. This is the CPU image — for NVIDIA GPU acceleration install OpenScrub-Nvidia instead. Best-effort redaction: always review output before sharing.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: redaction blur privacy PII PHI face license plate anonymize deface

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: HTTPS web interface (self-signed certificate — your browser warns once, or install your own cert in App settings).

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Jobs (contains the sensitive uploads and reports!), review decisions, certificates, zones, downloaded models, encryption vault. Everything OpenScrub stores lives here — the container itself is disposable.

Config.Name: Access token (optional)
Config.Description: Leave empty for open access on your LAN, or set a secret to require it on every request (recommended if others use your network). The WebUI URL becomes https://ip:8384/?token=SECRET on first visit.

Config.Name: Media
Config.Description: Optional read-only mount of your media share so the 'path on the server' picker can process files without uploading them through the browser.

Config.Name: OPENSCRUB_MEDIA_ROOT
Config.Description: Confines the server-path picker to this directory tree. Leave set to /media to match the Media mount; clear it to allow any container path.
